title: Nvidia launches RTX Spark chip to power Windows PCs with 128GB memory and AI agents
summary: Nvidia's new RTX Spark chip will enable Windows laptops to run AI agents locally, allowing users to automate tasks and use natural language. The chip features a 20-core Grace CPU and Blackwell RTX GPU, offering up to 1 petaflop of AI performance and 128GB of memory.

title: Arm says ByteDance, Oracle use its data centre CPU chips
summary: Speaking at ​the annual ​Computex conference in Taipei, Arm CEO ‌Rene Haas said ​ByteDance and ​Oracle ⁠are customers of AGI central processing units (CPUs).

title: China adds data and AI to trade secret rules to block leaks
summary: Effective Monday, the Regulations on Trade Secret Protection mark the first time Chinese law protects such digital assets as proprietary secrets, according to state broadcaster China Central Television.

title: Anthropic IPO filing ratifies Wall Street's AI obsession
summary: Tech stocks continue to lead ​the Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 in a record-breaking market rally, while Elon Musk's SpaceX is set to price its ​IPO this month and Anthropic rival OpenAI, led by Sam Altman, is waiting on deck.

title: AI-driven labour displacement risks to remain low in near term, Bridgewater says
summary: Adoption remains limited, with fewer ​than 20% of US firms reporting AI use in any business function over ​a two-week period, concentrated ⁠largely in ‌information, technology and professional ​services, Bridgewater ​said citing Census Bureau data.

title: China's Zhipu AI plans to apply for Shanghai's Sci-Tech Board listing
summary: One of the leading players in China's crowded artificial intelligence sector, Zhipu AI, said the proposed issuance of A shares, each with a ‌nominal value ⁠of 0.10 ⁠yuan, would represent between 2% and 8% of the company's total share capital, or 9.1 million to ​38.8 million shares.

title: Florida sues OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, claiming company concealed serious risks of ChatGPT
summary: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier said during a news conference that the company suppressed internal safety warnings and deceived users about the true nature and dangers of the product. He said Florida was the first state to sue OpenAI.

title: SEC defends Musk settlement over Twitter, saying it reflects 'compromises'
summary: In a filing in the Washington, D.C. federal court, the SEC also said in a footnote that the settlement if approved will allow Musk to publicly deny its accusations, reflecting a recent policy ‌change governing defendants ⁠who settle ⁠enforcement actions.

title: Nvidia CEO says has capacity to supply robust CPU and GPU growth
summary: Huang ​was ​speaking ⁠at Nvidia's GTC press conference ​during Computex week ​in Taipei a day after the $5 trillion ‌chip giant unveiled a ​new ​chip ⁠that processes artificial intelligence capabilities directly.

title: HPE lifts forecast past 2028 goals on robust AI demand, shares surge 36%
summary: HPE, ⁠which competes with Dell and Super Micro Computer, is benefiting as customers increasingly buy its servers and networking products to power AI ​applications such as ChatGPT, amid higher memory chip prices.
